channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Corbin Burnes and Arizona Diamondbacks agree to $210 million, 6-year deal, AP source says
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Corbin Burnes and the Arizona Diamondbacks have agreed to a $210 million, six-year contract, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press late Friday night. The person spoke to the AP on condition of anonymity because the deal was pending a successful physical.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McCord throws for 453 yards, 5 TDs in No. 22 Syracuse's 52-35 Holiday Bowl win over Washington State
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Kyle McCord threw for 453 yards and five touchdowns to break Deshaun Watson's Atlantic Coast Conference season passing record and lead No. 22 Syracuse to a 52-35 victory over depleted-yet-scrappy Washington State in the Holiday Bowl on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: BERNIE WILSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Syracuse defeats Washington State in DIRECTV Holiday Bowl
item.description: Syracuse quarterback Kyle McCord put on a stellar performance Friday to help his team win the DIRECTV Holiday Bowl at Snapdragon Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Stars forward Mason Marchment taken to hospital after getting hit in face by puck
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Stars forward Mason Marchment was helped off the ice during Friday night’s game against the Minnesota Wild and taken to a hospital after being hit in the face by a puck. Dallas coach Pete DeBoer said Marchment is OK, but was not sure how long he’ll be sidelined.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 2 sailor deaths result in muted celebrations on winning yacht in Sydney to Hobart race
item.description: Champagne corks often pop and loud, boisterous cheers are usually heard around Constitution Dock when the Sydney to Hobart line honors winner finishes in the Tasmanian state capital.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS PASSA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel dies from cancer at age 78
item.description: Greg Gumbel, a longtime CBS sportscaster who broke barriers during his career calling some of the biggest sporting events, has died from cancer, according to a statement from family released by the network on Friday. He was 78.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vanderbilt beats Georgia Tech 35-27 in Birmingham Bowl for first postseason win in 11 years
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — Diego Pavia and Vanderbilt delivered another victory to end a surprising season. Pavia threw three touchdown passes and ran for two more scores and Vanderbilt secured its first winning season since 2013 with a 35-27 victory over Georgia Tech on Friday in the Birmingham Bowl.
item.dc:creator: JOHN ZENOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Miami basketball coach Jim Larrañaga left 'exhausted' by NIL's impact on college sports
item.description: The longtime Hurricanes coach said his decision to abruptly step down from his position was partly due to his lack of interest in contending with NIL.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kings fire coach Mike Brown less than halfway through his 3rd season
item.description: The Sacramento Kings have fired coach Mike Brown less than halfway through his third season with the team mired in a five-game losing streak.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dubow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Athletics president Dave Kaval will resign from organization in the coming days
item.description: Athletics president Dave Kaval will resign from the organization after being the public face of the organization's planned move from Oakland to Las Vegas. Kaval has been president of the A's for the past eight years and will step down from the role on Dec.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Avalanche reward newly acquired goalie Blackwood with 5-year, $26.25 million contract extension
item.description: It didn’t take goalie Mackenzie Blackwood long to establish himself as the Colorado Avalanche’s starter for the present and future after signing a five-year $26.25 million contract extension on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aggies bounce back with 75-68 win over St. Mary's
item.description: MORAGA, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - Coming off its first loss of the season, the Utah State men’s basketball (11-1, 1-0 MW) got back in the win column Sunday night, closing out non-conference play with a 75-68 win over Saint Mary’s at the UCU Pavilion in Moraga.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bucs' Cody Mauch adjusting to and enjoying Tampa Bay's warm winters
item.description: Bucs' offensive lineman Cody Mauch is in his second year in the NFL, which means he is spending another Christmas in paradise.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jared Goff throws 3 TD passes as Lions beat Bears 34-17 for franchise-record 13th win
item.description: Jared Goff and the Detroit Lions locked in from the start and delivered a bounce-back performance. It came with a few team records, too.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: High-scoring Lions' fast start too much for Bears, Chicago loses 9th in a row
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Bears spotted the Detroit Lions an early 20-0 lead and despite catching their groove midway through the game, Chicago came up short for the second time this season against Detroit.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Bears vs. Lions: Injuries, inactives and key matchups for Sunday's game at Soldier Field
item.description: UPDATE: The Bears and Lions have announced their inactives for Sunday's game. See INJURY REPORT below. CHICAGO — The Bears are "playing out the string," as they say, eliminated from NFL playoff contention with three games still left to play this season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Tiger Woods and son Charlie share the lead at PNC Championship in Orlando
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Tiger Woods and 15-year-old son Charlie ran off five straight birdies on the back nine Saturday for a 13-under 59 in the scramble format, giving them a share of the lead in the PNC Championship in Woods' first competition since back surgery in September.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lamar Jackson and the Ravens draw even atop the AFC North, beating the 1st-place Steelers 34-17
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Lamar Jackson was solid in Baltimore's biggest game of the season so far. Then, after one of the star quarterback's rare slip-ups, one of his teammates delivered the play of the day.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz win back-to-back games for first time this season
item.description: BROOKLYN, N.Y. (ABC4 Sports) - For the first time since April, the Utah Jazz are on a winning streak. Lauri Markkanen scored 21 points, Collin Sexton and Svi Mykhailiuk each had 18 points, and the Utah Jazz beat the Brooklyn Nets 105-94 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Northwestern demolishes DePaul in crosstown showdown
item.description: EVANSTON, Ill. (WGN) — An early blitz on the offensive end and playmaking down the stretch from Brooks Barnhizer and Nick Martinelli helped seal a convincing win for Northwestern over DePaul in the yearly crosstown showdown between the two programs.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB legend, dies at 65: Reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB's all-time stolen base leader, dies at 65: reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WGN 9

item.title: NBA fines Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla $35,000 for postgame comments toward officials
item.description: The NBA fined the Boston Celtics coach $35,000 on Saturday “for aggressively pursuing and directing inappropriate language toward a game official” at the end of his team's loss to the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
